SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.allocation

Class AllocationUncertaintyEstimator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.allocation.AllocationUncertaintyEstimator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AllocationUncertaintyEstimator extends Object implements Serializable
Closed-form first-order uncertainty propagation for the linear recovery-factor allocation network.

Because the per-source allocation v_k = (I - A_k)^-1 b_k is linear in the metered source injections, metering-noise covariance propagates analytically:

Sigma_v_k = J_k * Sigma_b_k * J_k^T with J_k = (I - A_k)^-1.

This class implements the common case of independent per-source metering, i.e. a diagonal input covariance Sigma_b_k. For each component k, source s entering at node e_s with measured-injection standard deviation sigma_{s,k}, and custody outlet j produced by node w with frozen split factor f_w(j,k), the variance of the allocated component flow is

Var(alloc(s, j, k)) = f_w(j, k)^2 * (J_k[w, e_s])^2 * sigma_{s,k}^2.

Per-source per-outlet variances on the totalised molar and mass flows follow by summing independent component contributions. Standard deviations and one-sigma molar/mass intervals are exposed by AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult.

Algorithmic cost is the same O(K N^3) + O(K N^2 S) as the deterministic solve in LinearAllocationSolver: one LU per component, one back-substitution per source. No flash or equation-of-state evaluation is performed.

Correlated metering, frozen-split-factor uncertainty, and sensitivity-corrected drift terms are left for future work; see the accompanying paper for the derivation.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

    static class 
    AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult
    Result of an uncertainty-propagation run: per-source, per-custody, per-component variances of the allocated component flow plus aggregated totals.

  • Constructor Details

    • AllocationUncertaintyEstimator

      public AllocationUncertaintyEstimator()

  • Method Details

    • propagate

      public AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult propagate(SourceAllocator allocator, double[][] injectionVariance)
      Propagates independent metering noise on each source's per-component injection through the proxy network. Convenience overload that pulls source list, custody outlets, and entry-node indices from the allocator.
      Parameters:
      allocator - the configured allocator; must have a base case set and at least one source
      injectionVariance - per-source per-component variance of the injection in (mole/sec)^2; must be non-null and shape [S][K]
      Returns:
      an AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult with per-source per-custody per-component variances

    • propagate

      public AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult propagate(AllocationNetwork network, int[] sourceEntryUnits, List<CustodyOutlet> custodyOutlets, String[] sourceNames, String[] custodyNames, ProductType[] custodyTypes, String[] componentNames, double[] molarMass, double[][] injectionVariance)
      Propagates independent metering noise through the proxy network with explicit topology and label arrays. Lower-level entry point used by propagate(SourceAllocator, double[][]).
      Parameters:
      network - the assembled proxy network; must be non-null
      sourceEntryUnits - zero-based node index where each source enters the network, length S; must be non-null
      custodyOutlets - custody outlets in result order; must be non-null and length C
      sourceNames - source labels in result order; must be non-null and length S
      custodyNames - custody outlet labels in result order; must be non-null and length C
      custodyTypes - custody product types in result order; must be non-null and length C
      componentNames - component names on the master slate; must be non-null and length K
      molarMass - component molar masses in kg/kmol; must be non-null and length K
      injectionVariance - per-source per-component variance in (mole/sec)^2; must be non-null and shape [S][K]
      Returns:
      an AllocationUncertaintyEstimator.UncertaintyResult with per-source per-custody per-component variances

    • setNegativeClipTolerance

      public void setNegativeClipTolerance(double negativeClipTolerance)
      Sets the magnitude below which a tiny negative variance from round-off is clipped to zero.
      Parameters:
      negativeClipTolerance - the clip tolerance; must be non-negative

    • collectSources

      private static List<AllocationSource> collectSources(SourceAllocator allocator)
      Collects the configured (or auto-detected) sources from an allocator. Mirrors the same fallback rule used by SourceAllocator.allocate(): if no sources are registered, auto-detect them from external feed streams.
      Parameters:
      allocator - the allocator; must be non-null with a base case set
      Returns:
      the source list in result order

    • collectCustody

      private static List<CustodyOutlet> collectCustody(SourceAllocator allocator)
      Collects the configured (or auto-detected) custody outlets from an allocator. Mirrors the same fallback rule used by SourceAllocator.allocate(): if no outlets are registered, auto-detect them from terminal product streams.
      Parameters:
      allocator - the allocator; must be non-null with a base case set
      Returns:
      the custody outlet list in result order

    • toSimple

      private static org.ejml.simple.SimpleMatrix toSimple(double[][] a)
      Converts a primitive matrix to an EJML SimpleMatrix.
      Parameters:
      a - the matrix; must be non-null and rectangular
      Returns:
      the equivalent SimpleMatrix

    • isFinite

      private static boolean isFinite(org.ejml.simple.SimpleMatrix m)
      Checks whether all entries of a matrix are finite.
      Parameters:
      m - the matrix; must be non-null
      Returns:
      true if every entry is finite
